items.body:
­¤ö┤ **Design Issue** ŌĆö `AzureSupportedClouds` uses a closed template literal union type

```ts
export type AzureSupportedClouds = `${AzureClouds}`;
```

A closed template literal type over an enum means the type cannot be extended as new cloud values are added by the service. It also breaks forward-compatibility for SDK consumers. The recommended pattern is to keep the type open:

```ts
export type AzureSupportedClouds = string;
```

**Fix:** Update the TypeSpec definition or emitter configuration so this type resolves to `string` and regenerate.

­¤ö┤ **Design Issue** ŌĆö `AzureClouds` enum members use `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`

```ts
export enum AzureClouds {
    AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD = "AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD",
    AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD = "AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD",
    AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT = "AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT"
}
```

Per Azure SDK guidelines, enum members should use `PascalCase`. The expected shape is:

```ts
export enum AzureClouds {
    AzureChinaCloud = "AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD",
    AzurePublicCloud = "AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD",
    AzureUsGovernment = "AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT"
}
```

**Fix:** Add a `@clientName` decorator in the TypeSpec source to override the generated member names, then regenerate. Report this pattern to [autorest.typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) if it recurs across packages.

­¤ö┤ **Tool Issue** ŌĆö `KnownVersions.V20210808` has a synthetic `V` prefix

```ts
export enum KnownVersions {
    V20210808 = "2021-08-08"
}
```

TypeScript identifiers cannot start with a digit, so the generator prepends `V` to the date-based version string. The resulting name `V20210808` is non-idiomatic and surprising to SDK consumers.

**Fix:** Add a `@clientName("Ga20210808")` (or a more descriptive name) decorator on the enum member in the TypeSpec source to produce a valid PascalCase identifier, then regenerate. Report this to [autorest.typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues).

3 issues found in `@azure/arm-alertprocessingrules` (v1.0.0-beta.1, API version 2021-08-08): two design issues and one tool issue ŌĆö all in the `arm-alertprocessingrules-node.api.md` API surface file. CI is not yet fully evaluated (the review agent job is still in-progress for this run). The PR is a new package initial release.

<details>
